expectations.text: Recommends adding to ignored_columns before dropping, even if column appears unreferenced
expectations.evidence: Deploy 1 is explicitly adding ignored_columns as safety net.

expectations.text: Explains that ActiveRecord caches column names at startup (the reason for ignored_columns)
expectations.evidence: Explains AR caches table schema at boot; cached queries reference dropped column causing PG::UndefinedColumn.

expectations.text: Shows the migration with safety_assured { remove_column }
expectations.evidence: Migration shown is just remove_column :contacts, :legacy_notes, :text without safety_assured wrapping. strong_migrations would block this.

expectations.text: Describes a 2-deploy sequence (deploy ignored_columns, then drop column)
Mentions removing the ignored_columns entry after the column is dropped
expectations.evidence: Cleanup section says to remove the ignored_columns line in a subsequent change.

suggestions: The missing safety_assured is a real gap — on a project with strong_migrations installed (like Wealthbox), this migration would fail CI. The skill makes this explicit.
eval_feedback.overall: 4/5 pass without skill. Key miss: safety_assured is required on projects using strong_migrations.
